title: Rhyacophila
taxonomy: Rhyacophila is a genus of caddisflies, which are a type of insect in the order Trichoptera. These insects are typically found near streams, rivers, and lakes, where they feed on algae and small aquatic organisms. The genus Rhyacophila is characterized by its distinctive elongated bodies and long, slender abdomens. Rhyacophila species are often found in fast-flowing water, where they can be seen clinging to rocks and vegetation. Rhyacophila caddisflies play an important role in aquatic ecosystems as both predators and prey for other organisms.
